release_review.summary: Flower settles hosted terminal pending tool activity through published Floret pending-settlement and polling-repeat contracts without changing the Runtime Service compatibility window.
checked_surfaces: Runtime Service protocol_version remains redeven-runtime-v1; terminal pending-tool settlement is internal to Flower's AI runtime and Floret public runtime APIs rather than a runtime-service protocol version change
Floret is consumed only from the published github.com/floegence/floret v0.3.75 module with GOWORK=off validation and no replace, go.work, package-manager link, or local sibling path wiring
Terminal process ownership remains Redeven-local: process start/read/write/terminate, output tails, exit state, and local access checks still use existing Flower appserver surfaces

Completed pending terminal processes call Floret SettlePendingTool and apply the returned ThreadTurnProjection before writing Redeven diagnostic audit rows, so Flower UI activity stays sourced from Floret projection rather than ai_tool_calls or run events

terminal.read polling metadata uses the published Floret repeat_policy and progress_token contracts inside tool definitions/results; no Env App, Desktop, gateway, or Runtime Service wire field is added
Desktop attach/start, Local UI health, startup reports, sys.ping, Env App maintenance UI, SSH runtime bootstrap, gateway lifecycle, and Runtime Service control methods are unchanged
release_review.same_window_rationale: The compatibility window is unchanged because the feature is implemented inside Flower's AI runtime integration with the published Floret runtime module and does not add or change Runtime Service protocol messages. The Runtime Service snapshot protocol remains redeven-runtime-v1, Desktop-managed startup still uses structured machine presentation and startup reports, sys.ping and attach semantics are unchanged, Env App maintenance UI and gateway lifecycle are unchanged, and SSH/runtime bootstrap compatibility does not require a newer Desktop or Runtime binary pair.
